summary: Iter_15: Both fixes verified end-to-end. (1) New public GET /api/companies/lookup/{code} works: valid code returns {company_id, name, company_code}, unknown code returns 404 with 'not recognised' message, case-insensitive normalisation OK, no sensitive fields (office_lat/lng/_id) leak. Regression: /api/companies/by-code/{code} still requires auth (401 without token). (2) Employee signup happy path is now UNBLOCKED: filled Step 1 (phone=+919999778866, PIN=234567, company_code=A41C85 seeded via super admin), signup-next advanced to Step 2 with NO 'Not Found' error, submitted Step 2 with name='Test Signup' -> signup-done shown -> signup-goto-login navigated back to /pin-login, then pin-login with new phone/PIN succeeded and correctly routed pending employee to /pin-change (pin_must_change=true). No red-screen, no crash. (3) Logout residue fix VERIFIED: signed in as super admin, changed PIN, went to /profile, tapped logout -> landing (URL='/') rendered with testID=landing-screen; 'Maximum update depth exceeded' count = 0; 'REPLACE not handled' count = 0. Cleanup: test employee (+919999778866) deleted, test company (TEST_Iter15_Company) deleted, super admin PIN reset to 246810 with pin_must_change=true, pin_fail_count=0, pin_locked_until=null; final POST /api/auth/admin-pin-login with 246810 -> 200.

react_native_issues.issue: Pre-existing deprecation warning: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow".' Not blocking.

action_items: Both critical issues from iter_14 are RESOLVED. No pending action items from this iteration.
OPTIONAL (carried over from iter_14): Consider blocking pin-login when approval_status='pending' (currently a pending employee still gets a session_token and is routed to /pin-change).
OPTIONAL (carried over from iter_14): server.py is ~2311 lines; consider splitting into modules (auth.py, companies.py, attendance.py, etc.) — well above the 700-line guideline.
critical_code_review_comments: GET /api/companies/lookup/{code} correctly projects only {company_id, name, company_code} — no sensitive fields leaked. Good.
AuthContext.logout: hard-navigate to '/' via window.location.assign BEFORE any state setter on web is the right ordering; verified no 'Maximum update depth' warning fires now.

Employee signup allows creation of an approval_status='pending' user and /auth/pin-login still returns 200 + session_token for that user. This is functional (they hit /pin-change first) but if the intent is to fully block pending employees at login, add explicit approval gate.

success_rate.backend: 100% (4/4 lookup tests + all iter_14 pytest still pass)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (signup happy path Step1->Step2->Done->pin-login->pin-change all pass; logout returns to landing with no console errors)

seed_data_creation: Created test company TEST_Iter15_Company (code=A41C85) and test employee (+919999778866, PIN=234567, name='Test Signup') during E2E signup. BOTH deleted at end. Super admin PIN reset to 246810, pin_must_change=true, pin_fail_count=0, pin_locked_until=null. Verified via POST /api/auth/admin-pin-login -> 200.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter_14 blockers are fully closed. Public /api/companies/lookup/{code} contract is exercised in /app/backend/tests/test_lookup_iter15.py and is stable. If future signup/login flows are added, reuse test_employee_signup_iter14.py + test_lookup_iter15.py + cleanup_iter15.py as regression baselines. Super admin credentials remain: sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com / 246810 (with pin_must_change=true, so first login will land on /pin-change).
rca_of_issue: No new bugs. Both bugs identified in iter_14 are RESOLVED and verified: (a) missing public lookup endpoint is now present at server.py:1303 returning {company_id, name, company_code} or 404 'not recognised'; (b) AuthContext.logout web branch now calls window.location.assign('/') FIRST and returns, avoiding the transient setUser(null) render loop that caused the 'Maximum update depth exceeded' warning in iter_14.
